nameMeanings.name: St
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Americanized spelling of French   St  .   Denis  .

variants.name: Denis
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Camille, Jacques, Armand, Marcel, Andre, Pierre, Serge, Yvan, Alphonse, Edouard, Francois, Gaetane.  Spanish Jose, Carlos, Jorge, Roberto, Angel, Francisco, Joaquin, Juan, Manuel, Miguel, Ramon, Rolando.

French, Walloon, Breton, West Indian (mainly Haiti), German, Dutch, English, and Portuguese; Spanish and Galician (Denís): from the personal name Denis, Spanish and Galician Denís, from Latin Dionysius ‘(follower) of Dionysos’ (see Dennis ). Compare Dennee .
Ukrainian and Rusyn: variant transliteration of   Denys  , a cognate of 1 above.

nameMeanings.name: Omer
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Ahmed, Mohamed, Mohammed, Tarig, Mohammad, Murad, Abdirahman, Abdul, Abdulla, Abdulrahim, Ali, Amin.

variants.name: Omar
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Arabic/Muslim Mohammed, Mohamed, Ali, Omar, Ahmed, Ahmad, Hassan, Abdul, Husam, Khalid, Mohammad, Mohd.

Muslim: from the Arabic personal name Omar of uncertain etymology, found almost exclusively among Sunni Muslims. It is thought to be related to Arabic ʿāmir ‘prosperous, full of life, large, substantial’ (see Amir 2). The root word ʿumr means ‘life’. ʿUmar bin al-Khaṭṭāb was the second of the ‘rightly guided’ caliphs (ruled 634–44); he was known by the title al-Fārūq ‘the distinguisher’, i.e. one who distinguishes between truth and falsehood. Compare Omer and Umar .
Catalan: topographic name for someone who lived by a group of elm trees, from the collective form of om ‘elm’.

nameMeanings.name: Maud
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Medieval vernacular form of Matilda . This form was characteristically Low German (i.e. including medieval Dutch and Flemish). The wife of William the Conqueror , who bore this name, was the daughter of Baldwin , Count of Flanders. In Flemish and Dutch the letter -t- was generally lost when it occurred between vowels, giving forms such as Ma(h)auld. Maud or Matilda was also the name of the daughter ( 1102–67 ) of Henry I of England; she was married early in life to the Holy Roman Emperor Henry V , and later disputed the throne of England with her cousin Stephen . In 1128 she married Geoffrey , Count of Anjou. A medieval chronicler commented, ‘she was a good woman, but she had little bliss with him’. The name Maud became quite common in England in the 19th century, when its popularity was influenced in part by Tennyson's poem Maud, published in 1855 , but has not been much used since the early decades of the 20th century.

en.lifeSummary: When Maud St Omer was born in 1110, in Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, her father, Guillaume de Saint-Omer, was 26 and her mother, Melisende Picquigny, was 35. She had at least 1 son and 8 daughters with Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand. She died in 1169, at the age of 59.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Maud St Omer est née en 1110, à Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, France, son père, Guillaume de Saint-Omer, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Melisende Picquigny, avait 35 ans. Elle a eu au moins 1 fils et 8 filles avec Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand. Elle est décédée en 1169, à l’âge de 59 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Maud St Omer 1110 in Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Frankreich geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Guillaume de Saint-Omer 26 und ihre Mutter Melisende Picquigny 35. Sie hatte mit Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand mindestens 1 Sohn und 8 Töchter. Sie starb 1169 im Alter von 59 Jahren.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Maud St Omer è nata il nel 1110 nel Saint-Omer, Pas-de-Calais, Nord-Pas-de-Calais, Francia, sua padre, Guillaume de Saint-Omer, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Melisende Picquigny, aveva 35 anni. Lei avuto almeno 1 figlio e 8 figlie con Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand. Morì nel 1169, all'età di 59 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Maud St Omerが1110年にフランスノード-プアス-デ-カレイスプアス-デ-カレイスサイント-オマーで生まれた時，父Guillaume de Saint-Omerは26歳，母Melisende Picquignyは35歳であった。彼女はArnould de Guisnes I Comte Gandとの間に少なくとも1人の息子と8人の娘をもうけた。彼女は1169年に59歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Maud St Omer(이)가 1110년 프랑스 노르드-파스-데-캘레이 파스-데-캘레이 세인트-머에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Guillaume de Saint-Omer(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Melisende Picquigny(은)는 35세였다. 그녀는 Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1169년 59세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Maud St Omer родилась в 1110 г. в местности сеинт-омир, пас-де-калеи, норд-пас-де-калеи, Франция, её отцу по имени Guillaume de Saint-Omer было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Melisende Picquigny, 35. У неё был по крайней мере 1 сын и 8 дочерей от Arnould de Guisnes I Comte Gand. Она умерла в 1169 г. в возрасте 59 лет.
